_meta.description: FloraLexicon Tier 2 region definitions. Each region has a country roster; every Tier 2 plant in that region must have names for every country listed. Countries may belong to multiple regions (e.g. MX in north_america and central_america); plants may not. Every country_code must exist in canonical/countries.json.

andean.name: Andean
andean.description: The Andean cultural-ecological zone spanning the high Andes from Venezuela to northern Chile/Argentina.

amazon.name: Amazon Basin
amazon.description: The Amazon basin, shared ethnobotanically across multiple South American countries.

caribbean.name: Caribbean
caribbean.description: Island nations and cultural zones of the Caribbean. Shares some ethnobotany with Central America.

central_america.name: Central America
central_america.description: Isthmus from southern Mexico through Panama.

east_africa.name: East Africa
east_africa.description: Horn of Africa and East African region.

east_asia.name: East Asia
east_asia.description: China, Japan, Korea, Taiwan. Shared traditional medicine lineage via Chinese materia medica.

eastern_europe.name: Eastern Europe
eastern_europe.description: Central and eastern European nations with shared Slavic and Central European herbal traditions.

mediterranean.name: Mediterranean
mediterranean.description: Mediterranean basin including southern Europe, North Africa, and the Levant. Shared culinary-medicinal tradition.

nordic.name: Nordic
nordic.description: Nordic countries with shared Germanic/Scandinavian herbal tradition.

north_america.name: North America
north_america.description: US, Canada, and northern Mexico. Shared commercial herbal market with Indigenous ethnobotanical substrate.

oceania.name: Oceania
oceania.description: Australia and New Zealand. Distinct Indigenous ethnobotany plus European settler tradition.

south_asia.name: South Asia
south_asia.description: Indian subcontinent. Ayurvedic and related traditional medicine systems.

southeast_asia.name: Southeast Asia
southeast_asia.description: ASEAN region. Diverse traditions with significant Chinese and Indian influence.

southern_africa.name: Southern Africa
southern_africa.description: Southern African region with shared ethnobotany and regulated traditional medicine in South Africa.

southern_cone.name: Southern Cone
southern_cone.description: Argentina, Chile, Uruguay, Paraguay, southern Brazil. Shared Spanish/Portuguese/Guaraní ethnobotany.

west_africa.name: West Africa
west_africa.description: West African region with shared ethnobotany across anglophone and francophone nations.

western_europe.name: Western Europe
western_europe.description: Western European nations with shared Germanic/Romance herbal and pharmacopoeial tradition.
